macro_segments.title: The Fixed Length of Adar and Its Implications for Rosh Chodesh Nissan

micro_segments.title: Introduction: The Question of Whether Adar Is Always Twenty-Nine Days
Rav Nachman's Challenge: Why Do Witnesses Violate Shabbat for Nissan If Adar Is Always Twenty-Nine?
The Gemara's Response: The Mitzvah to Sanctify Based on Testimony
Rav Kahana's Objection: The Distinction Between Months Based on Practical Consequences
The Gemara's Conclusion: Adar Can Vary Between Twenty-Nine and Thirty Days

macro_segments.title: Ulla's Report: The Making of Elul into Thirty Days and the Benefits to Babylon

micro_segments.title: Ulla's Announcement: Elul Was Made Thirty Days in Eretz Yisrael
The Two Reasons for Adjusting the Calendar: Dead Bodies (Metaya) and Vegetables (Yarka)
The Difference Between the Two Reasons: Which Adjustments They Justify
Why Babylon Should Be Grateful: The Hotter Climate Makes These Concerns More Pressing

macro_segments.title: Can the Calendar Be Adjusted Independently of Witnesses? The Debate Over 'Kabua' Versus 'Al Pi Hare'iyah'

micro_segments.title: The Question: Can We Add Days to Months for a Need, Like We Add Months to Years?
The Verse as Limit: 'This Month Is For You—See and Sanctify' Requires Testimony First
Rava's Distinction: Delaying (Le'avro) Versus Early Sanctification (Lekadsho)
The Limits of Manipulation: We Can Delay but Not Advance, and We Cannot Coerce False Testimony
Rav Yehuda Nesi'ah's Report: Rabbi Yochanan Taught the Opposite—We Can Coerce Testimony for Early Sanctification
Abaye's Resolution: The Rule Differs for Nissan and Tishrei Versus Other Months
Rava's Alternative: The Statement Against Manipulation Is the Minority View of Rabbi Meir
Rav Dimi's Teaching: It Is Better to Advance with False Testimony Than to Delay Visibly

macro_segments.title: The Astronomy of the Moon: Shmuel's Claim and the Secret of Intercalation (Sod Ha'Ibbur)

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's Boast: He Could Set the Calendar for All the Exile Based on Astronomy
Abba's Test: The Cryptic Teaching About Whether the Molad Occurs Before or After Chatzot
Rav Zeira's Instruction from Eretz Yisrael: Night and Day Must Both Belong to the Month
Rashi's Interpretation: The Visibility of the Old Moon as a Constraint on Calendar Manipulation
The Significance of Before or After Chatzot: Determining When the New Moon Will Be Visible
Rav Ashi's Purpose: Knowing Astronomy to Contradict False Witnesses
The Twenty-Four-Hour Concealment of the Moon: Different Duration for Old Versus New in Eretz Yisrael Versus Bavel
The Geographic Explanation: The Hour Difference Between Bavel and Eretz Yisrael Determines Whether the Molad Is Before or After Chatzot

topical_tags.term: Adar—whether fixed at twenty-nine days or variable
Rosh Chodesh Nissan—witnesses violating Shabbat
Elul—always twenty-nine days (challenged)
Kiddush al pi ha're'iyah—sanctification based on witnesses
Metaya (dead bodies)—reason to avoid Shabbat and Yom Kippur consecutive
Yarka (vegetables)—reason to avoid Shabbat and Yom Tov consecutive
Le'avro (delaying) versus lekadsho (advancing) sanctification
Rav Kahana—objection that flexibility undermines witness testimony
Rava bar Shmuel—teaching that calendar is not manipulated
Abaye—distinction between Nissan/Tishrei and other months
Rav Dimi—preference for early sanctification with false testimony
Shmuel—astronomer claiming ability to set entire calendar
Abba father of Rabbi Simlai—testing Shmuel on the secret of intercalation
Rav Zeira—transmitting astronomical rulings from Eretz Yisrael
Molad—the astronomical moment when the moon moves from conjunction
Chatzot (midday)—the critical time dividing whether new moon will be visible
Twenty-four-hour concealment—the moon invisible for one full day cycle
Six hours of old moon and eighteen hours of new moon (or vice versa)
Geographic difference: Bavel versus Eretz Yisrael in moon visibility
Sod ha'ibbur (secret of intercalation)—cryptic astronomical teachings
Rav Ashi—purpose of astronomy is to contradict false witnesses
